task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web interface is visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Perform a search for the keyword 'sudo'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar in EventLog Analyzer contains the word 'sudo' and search results (log entries) are displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Set the search time filter to the last hour
task_completion.visual_evidence: A time filter dropdown, badge, or label in the EventLog Analyzer UI explicitly shows 'Last 1 Hour', 'Last Hour', or an equivalent 60-minute timeframe.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the search results as a PDF
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export menu or dialog is open in EventLog Analyzer with 'PDF' selected as the export format, or a PDF generation loading screen is visible.
A 'Save As' dialog shows the directory '/home/ga/Documents' and the filename 'sudo_evidence.pdf', or a file manager/terminal window confirms the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual EventLog Analyzer UI being used for searching, not a text editor, mock webpage, or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to fake the PDF file
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'echo', 'touch', 'wkhtmltopdf', or 'enscript' being used to manually create or overwrite 'sudo_evidence.pdf'.

integrity.description: The PDF was generated via the application's built-in export feature
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the export/download button within the EventLog Analyzer interface rather than downloading a pre-existing file from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be exactly named 'sudo_evidence.pdf' and saved in '/home/ga/Documents/'. The search keyword is strictly 'sudo'. The underlying injected logs contain the keywords 'sudo', 'COMMAND', and 'root', which should be visible in the EventLog Analyzer search results before export.
